256 This routine allocates all the memory needed to contain information

264 int lx, ly, lz;

277 mymap.lz = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_z_];

289 if (!ThisTask)

291 printf("\n");

305 MPI_Send((void *)&mymap, sizeof(struct map), MPI_BYTE, 0, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
306 }

308 {
309 printf("\n");
310 fflush(stdout);
311 }
312
313 /* this is just to have a little margin */
314 memory.all += 10;
315
316 /* it tests than there is enough space to allocate all needed memory */
317 main_memory = (char *)calloc(memory.all, sizeof(char));
318 if (main_memory == 0x0)
319 {
320 printf("ERROR on task %d: I cannot allocate memory\n", ThisTask);
321 fflush(stdout);
322 return 1;
323 }

325
326 /* allocates main memory */
327 main_memory = (char *)calloc(memory.first_allocated, sizeof(char));

329 {
330 printf("ERROR on taks %d: I cannot allocate memory after first successful attempt\n", ThisTask);
331 fflush(stdout);
332 return 1;
333 }
334
335 /* sets pointers to the allocated memory */
336 products = (product_data *)main_memory;
337 count_memory = MyGrids[0].total_local_size * sizeof(product_data);
338 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);

340 {
341 kdensity[igrid] = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);

345#ifdef TWO_LPT
346 kvector_2LPT = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
347 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
348 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
349 source_2LPT = kvector_2LPT;
350#ifdef THREE_LPT
351 kvector_3LPT_1 = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
352 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
353 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
354 kvector_3LPT_2 = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
355 count_memory += MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
356 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
357 source_3LPT_1 = kvector_3LPT_1;
358 source_3LPT_2 = kvector_3LPT_2;
359#endif
360#endif
361 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
362 {
363 for (i = 0; i < 6; i++)
364 {
365 second_derivatives[igrid][i] = (double *)(main_memory + count_memory);
366 count_memory += MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size * sizeof(double);
367 ALIGN_MEMORY_BLOCK(count_memory);
368 }
369 for (i = 0; i < 3; i++)
370 first_derivatives[igrid][i] = second_derivatives[igrid][i];
371 density[igrid] = second_derivatives[igrid][0];
372 }
373 // for (igrid=0; igrid<Ngrids; igrid++)
374 // {
375 // seedtable[igrid] = (unsigned int*)(main_memory + count_memory);
376 // count_memory += MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_y_] * sizeof(unsigned int);
377 // }
378 //
379 /* allocates fft vectors */
380 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
381 {
382 rvector_fft[igrid] = pfft_alloc_real(MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft);
383 cvector_fft[igrid] = pfft_alloc_complex(MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft / 2);
384 // printf("Task %d has got alignment for grid %d [ %llu %llu %llu - %llu %llu %llu ]\n",
385 // ThisTask, igrid, (unsigned long long int)rvector_fft[igrid] % 256, (unsigned long long int)rvector_fft[igrid] % 128, (unsigned long long int)rvector_fft[igrid] % 64,
386 //(unsigned long long int)cvector_fft[igrid] % 256, (unsigned long long int)cvector_fft[igrid] % 128, (unsigned long long int)cvector_fft[igrid] % 64);
387
388 if (rvector_fft[igrid] == 0x0 || cvector_fft[igrid] == 0x0)
389 {
390 printf("ERROR on taks %d: I cannot allocate memory for fft vectors\n", ThisTask);
391 fflush(stdout);
392 return 1;
393 }
394 }
395
396 if (!ThisTask)
397 printf("[%s] Memory has been successfully allocated\n", fdate());
398
399#ifdef VERBOSE
400
401 last = main_memory + memory.first_allocated;
402#ifndef TWO_LPT
403 bsize = 3 + 3 * Ngrids;
404#else
405 bsize = 4 + 3 * Ngrids;
406#endif
407 bsize *= 2;
408 bcast = (size_t *)malloc(bsize * sizeof(size_t));
409
410 n = 0;
411 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)kdensity[0] - (void *)products);
412 for (igrid = 1; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
413 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)kdensity[igrid] - (void *)kdensity[igrid - 1]);
414#ifdef TWO_LPT
415 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)kvector_2LPT - (void *)second_derivatives[Ngrids - 1][0]);
416 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)seedtable[0] - (void *)kvector_2LPT);
417#else
418 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)seedtable[0] - (void *)second_derivatives[Ngrids - 1][0]);
419#endif
420 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)second_derivatives[0][0] - (void *)kdensity[Ngrids - 1]);
421 for (igrid = 1; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
422 bcast[n++] = (size_t)((void *)second_derivatives[igrid][0] - (void *)second_derivatives[igrid - 1][0]);
423 /* for (igrid=1; igrid<Ngrids; igrid++) */
424 /* bcast[n++]=(size_t)((void*)seedtable[igrid]-(void*)seedtable[igrid-1]); */

426 bcast[n++] = (size_t)(last - (void *)seedtable[Ngrids - 1]);
427 bcast[n++] = (size_t)(last - (void *)products);
428 bcast[n++] = memory.fft;
429
430 bcast[n++] = memory.prods;
431 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
432 bcast[n++] = MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
433 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
434 bcast[n++] = 6 * MyGrids[igrid].total_local_size * sizeof(double);
435#ifdef TWO_LPT
436#ifndef THREE_LPT
437 bcast[n++] = MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
438#else
439 bcast[n++] = 3 * MyGrids[0].total_local_size_fft * sizeof(double);
440#endif
441#endif

443 bcast[n++] = MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_x_] * MyGrids[igrid].GSglobal[_y_] * sizeof(unsigned int);
444 bcast[n++] = count_memory;
445 bcast[n++] = memory.all;
446
447 if (!ThisTask)
448 {
449 printf("Map of memory allocations for fmax (in byte):\n");
450 printf(" TASK ");
451 printf(" PRODUCTS");
452 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
453 printf(" KDENS G.%d", igrid);
454#ifdef TWO_LPT
455 printf(" KVEC 2LPT ");
456#ifdef THREE_LPT
457 printf(" KVEC 3LPT ");
458#endif
459#endif
460 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
461 printf(" DERIV G.%d", igrid);
462 for (igrid = 0; igrid < Ngrids; igrid++)
463 printf(" SEEDS G.%d ", igrid);
464 printf(" MAIN MEMORY ");
465 printf("- FFT VECTORS / REQ. MEMORY\n");
466 }
467
468 for (i = 0; i < NTasks; i++)
469 {
470
471 if (!ThisTask)
472 {
473 if (i)
474 s = MPI_Recv((void *)bcast, bsize * sizeof(size_t), MPI_BYTE, i, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD, &status);
475 printf(" FOUND %5d", i);
476 for (n = 0; n < bsize / 2; n++)
477 printf(" %12zu", bcast[n]);
478 printf("\n");
479 printf(" PRED %5d", i);
480 for (n = bsize / 2; n < bsize; n++)
481 printf(" %12zu", bcast[n]);
482 printf("\n");
483 }
484 else if (ThisTask == i)
485 s = MPI_Send((void *)bcast, bsize * sizeof(size_t), MPI_BYTE, 0, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
486 }
487#endif
488
489 if (!ThisTask)
490 {
491 printf("\n");
492 fflush(stdout);
493 }
494 return 0;
495}

Definition at line 65 of file build_groups.c.

66{
67
68 /* The algorithm for group construction runs as follows:
69 loop on all collapsed particle
70 + for each particle check its neighbours
71 + if it is a peak of Fmax, make it a one-particle halo
72 + if it touches only one halo:
73 -> check if it should be accreted
74 -> if it should, accrete it
75 -> otherwise, tag it as filament
76 + if it touches more than one halo:
77 -> check if it should be accreted to one halo
78 -> if it should, choose the one it gets nearest to
79 -> then check if all the halo pairs should merge
80 -> if they should, merge them
81 -> if the particle was not accreted before, re-check it
82 -> otherwise, tag it as filament
83 + if it touches only filaments, tag it as filament
84 */
85
86 int merge[NV][NV], neigh[NV], fil_list[NV][4];
87 static int iout, nstep, nstep_p;
88 int nn, ifil, this_z, neigrp, nf, pos;
89 int iz, i1, j1, k1, skip;
90 int ig3, small, large, to_group, accgrp, ig1, ig2;
91 int accrflag, nmerge, peak_cond;
92 double ratio, best_ratio, d2, r2, cputmp;
93 int merge_flag;
94 int ibox, jbox, kbox;
95
96 static int last_z = 0;
97
98 /*
99 counters of various events:
100 0 number of peaks
101 i number of particles with i neighbours (i<=6)
102 7 number of accretion events
103 8 number of accretion events before checking a merger
104 9 number of accretion events after checking a merger
105 10 number of merging events
106 11 number of major mergers
107 12 number of filament particles
108 13 number of accreted filament particles
109 14 number of good halos
110 */
111
112 static unsigned long long counters[NCOUNTERS], all_counters[NCOUNTERS];
113
114#ifdef PLC
115 static int plc_started = 0, last_check_done = 0;
116 int irep, save, mysave, storex, storey, storez;
117 static double NextF_PLC, DeltaF_PLC;
118 double aa, bb, Fplc;
119#endif
120
121 if (first_call)
122 {
123 /* this part contains initializations that should be done at
124 first call */
125 ngroups = FILAMENT; /* group list starts from FILAMENTS */
126 /* filaments are not grouped */
127 for (i1 = 0; i1 <= FILAMENT; i1++)
128 {
129 groups[i1].point = -1;
130 groups[i1].bottom = -1;
131 groups[i1].good = 0;
132 }
133 iout = 0;
134
135 /* sets the counter to zero */
136 for (i1 = 0; i1 < NCOUNTERS; i1++)
137 {
138 counters[i1] = 0;
139 all_counters[i1] = 0;
140 }
141
142 if (!ThisTask)
143 printf("[%s] Starting the fragmentation process to redshift %7.4f\n", fdate(), zstop);
144
145 /* nstep is the number of collapsed particles that will be
146 checked by the code. In classic fragmentation this is the
147 number of particles that collapse by the end of the run. In
148 default fragmentation this selection is performed at
149 distribution time, so this is the number of stored particles.
150 */
151#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
152 nstep = 0;
153 while (frag[indices[nstep]].Fmax >= outputs.Flast)
154 nstep++;
155#else
156 nstep = subbox.Nstored;
157#endif
158 nstep_p = nstep / 20;
159
160#ifdef PLC
161 /* initialization of the root finding routine used by the PLC code */
162 cPLC.function = &condition_F;
163 brent = gsl_root_fsolver_brent;
164 solver = gsl_root_fsolver_alloc(brent);
165 DeltaF_PLC = 0.9; // CAPIRE COME FISSARLO
166 NextF_PLC = plc.Fstart * DeltaF_PLC;
167 brent_err = 1.e-2 * params.InterPartDist;
168 plc.Nstored = plc.Nstored_last = 0;
169#endif
170
171 first_call = 0;
172 }
173 else
174 {
175 if (!ThisTask)
176 printf("[%s] Restarting the fragmentation process to redshift %7.4f\n", fdate(), zstop);
177 }
178
179 /************************************************************************
180 START OF THE CYCLE ON COLLAPSED PARTICLES
181 ************************************************************************/
182 for (this_z = last_z; this_z < nstep; this_z++)
183 {
184 /* In classic fragmentation the particles must be addressed in
185 order of collapse time, while in default fragmentation they
186 are already in that order */
187#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
188 iz = indices[this_z];
189#else
190 iz = this_z;
191#endif
192
193#ifdef PLC
194 /* here it checks if it is time to write the stored PLC halos.
195 If it is the case, it waits for all the tasks to get to the
196 same point and writes the catalog up to this point
197 */
198 if (plc_started && frag[iz].Fmax < NextF_PLC && frag[iz].Fmax >= plc.Fstop)
199 {
200 if (!ThisTask)
201 printf("[%s] Syncing tasks for PLC... Nmax=%d, Nstored=%d, Nstored_last=%d, Fmax=%f\n", fdate(), plc.Nmax, plc.Nstored, plc.Nstored_last, frag[iz].Fmax);
202
203 /* each task checks if the plc buffer is full and it is
204 necessary to write the plc catalog. The criterion is that
205 you need at least space for SAFEPLC times the number of
206 halos that have been updated after last check */
207 mysave = (plc.Nmax - plc.Nstored < SAFEPLC * (plc.Nstored - plc.Nstored_last));
208
209 MPI_Reduce(&mysave, &save, 1, MPI_INT, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
210 MPI_Bcast(&save, 1, MPI_INT, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
211
212 if (!ThisTask)
213 printf("[%s] %d tasks require to store PLC halos at F=%6.3F...\n", fdate(), save, frag[iz].Fmax);
214
215 if (save)
216 {
217 /* Save PLC halos if at least one task asks to */
218 if (write_PLC(0))
219 return 1;
220 plc.Nstored = 0;
221 }
222
223 /* next update will be at this time */
224 NextF_PLC *= DeltaF_PLC;
225 plc.Nstored_last = plc.Nstored;
226 }
227#endif
228
229 /* More initializations */
230
231 neigrp = 0; /* number of neighbouring groups */
232 nf = 0; /* number of neighbouring filament points */
233 accrflag = 0; /* if =1 all the neighbouring filaments are accreted */
234 for (i1 = 0; i1 < NV; i1++)
235 neigh[i1] = 0; /* number of neighbours */
236
237 /*******************************************/
238 /* PARTICLE COORDINATES AND PEAK CONDITION */
239 /*******************************************/
240#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
241 INDEX_TO_COORD(iz, ibox, jbox, kbox, subbox.Lgwbl);
242#else
243 INDEX_TO_COORD(frag_pos[iz], ibox, jbox, kbox, subbox.Lgwbl);
244#endif
245
246 /* skips the peak condition if the point is at the border (and PBCs are not active) */
247 skip = 0;
248 if (!subbox.pbc[_x_] && (ibox == 0 || ibox == subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1))
249 ++skip;
250 if (!subbox.pbc[_y_] && (jbox == 0 || jbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1))
251 ++skip;
252 if (!subbox.pbc[_z_] && (kbox == 0 || kbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1))
253 ++skip;
254
255 particle_name =
256 COORD_TO_INDEX((long long)((ibox + subbox.stabl[_x_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]),
257 (long long)((jbox + subbox.stabl[_y_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]),
258 (long long)((kbox + subbox.stabl[_z_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]),
259 MyGrids[0].GSglobal);
260
261 good_particle = (ibox >= subbox.safe[_x_] && ibox < subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - subbox.safe[_x_] &&
262 jbox >= subbox.safe[_y_] && jbox < subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - subbox.safe[_y_] &&
263 kbox >= subbox.safe[_z_] && kbox < subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - subbox.safe[_z_]);
264
265 if (!skip)
266 {
267 peak_cond = 1;
268 /* checks whether the neighbouring particles collapse later */
269
270 for (nn = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
271 {
272 /* coordinates of the neighbouring particle */
273 switch (nn)
274 {
275 case 0:
276 i1 = (subbox.pbc[_x_] && ibox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1 : ibox - 1);
277 j1 = jbox;
278 k1 = kbox;
279 break;
280 case 1:
281 i1 = (subbox.pbc[_x_] && ibox == subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1 ? 0 : ibox + 1);
282 j1 = jbox;
283 k1 = kbox;
284 break;
285 case 2:
286 i1 = ibox;
287 j1 = (subbox.pbc[_y_] && jbox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1 : jbox - 1);
288 k1 = kbox;
289 break;
290 case 3:
291 i1 = ibox;
292 j1 = (subbox.pbc[_y_] && jbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1 ? 0 : jbox + 1);
293 k1 = kbox;
294 break;
295 case 4:
296 i1 = ibox;
297 j1 = jbox;
298 k1 = (subbox.pbc[_z_] && kbox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1 : kbox - 1);
299 break;
300 case 5:
301 i1 = ibox;
302 j1 = jbox;
303 k1 = (subbox.pbc[_z_] && kbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1 ? 0 : kbox + 1);
304 break;
305 }
306
307 /* accessing the neighbouring particle differs in
308 classic and default fragmentation: in classic
309 fragmentation the information on the neighbour is
310 immediately obtained, in standard fragmentation the
311 neighbour must be seeked in the particle list */
312#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
313 pos = COORD_TO_INDEX(i1, j1, k1, subbox.Lgwbl);
314 neigh[nn] = group_ID[pos];
315 peak_cond &= (frag[iz].Fmax > frag[pos].Fmax);
316#else
317 pos = find_location(i1, j1, k1);
318 if (pos >= 0)
319 {
320 neigh[nn] = group_ID[indices[pos]];
321 peak_cond &= (frag[iz].Fmax > frag[indices[pos]].Fmax);
322 }
323 else
324 neigh[nn] = 0;
325#endif
326
327 /* neighbouring filaments are stored separately */
328 if (neigh[nn] == FILAMENT)
329 {
330 neigh[nn] = 0;
331 fil_list[nf][0] = i1;
332 fil_list[nf][1] = j1;
333 fil_list[nf][2] = k1;
334#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
335 fil_list[nf][3] = pos;
336#else
337 fil_list[nf][3] = indices[pos];
338#endif
339 nf++;
340 }
341 }
342
343 /* Cleans the list of neighbouring groups removing duplicates */
344 clean_list(neigh);
345
346 /* Number of neighbouring groups */
347 for (nn = neigrp = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
348 if (neigh[nn] > FILAMENT)
349 neigrp++;
350
351 if (neigrp > 0 && good_particle)
352 counters[neigrp]++;
353
354#ifdef PLC
355 /* Past light cone on-the-fly reconstruction: */
356
357 /* is it time to reconstruct the PLC? */
358 if (frag[iz].Fmax < plc.Fstart && frag[iz].Fmax >= plc.Fstop)
359 {
360
361 /* check if this is the first call */
362 if (!plc_started)
363 {
364 plc_started = 1;
365 if (!ThisTask)
366 printf("[%s] Starting PLC reconstruction, Task 0 will store at most %d halos\n", fdate(), plc.Nmax);
367 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
368 }
369
370 /* PBCs are switched off for this check */
371 storex = subbox.pbc[_x_];
372 storey = subbox.pbc[_y_];
373 storez = subbox.pbc[_z_];
374 subbox.pbc[_x_] = subbox.pbc[_y_] = subbox.pbc[_z_] = 0;
375
376 /* the check is performed on all neighbouring groups */
377 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
378 {
379 /* is the group good and massive enough? */
380 if (neigh[ig1] > FILAMENT &&
381 groups[neigh[ig1]].good &&
382 groups[neigh[ig1]].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
383 {
384 thisgroup = neigh[ig1];
385
386 /* loop on replications */
387 /* this loop may be threaded or ported to GPUs,
388 though it's relatively fast */
389 for (irep = 0; irep < plc.Nreplications; irep++)
390 /* checks that the redshift falls in the
391 range of the replication */
392 if (!(frag[iz].Fmax > plc.repls[irep].F1 ||
393 groups[thisgroup].Flast < plc.repls[irep].F2))
394 {
395 replicate[0] = plc.repls[irep].i;
396 replicate[1] = plc.repls[irep].j;
397 replicate[2] = plc.repls[irep].k;
398 /* this computes the difference between
399 the group distance from the observer
400 and its comoving distance at that z */
401 bb = condition_PLC(frag[iz].Fmax);
402
403 /* in this unlikely case we catch the
404 group just on the PLC */
405 if (bb == 0.0)
406 {
407 if (store_PLC(frag[iz].Fmax))
408 return 1;
409 }
410 else if (bb > 0.)
411 {
412 /* if it is outside the PLC then check
413 whether it has just passed since
414 last check */
415 aa = condition_PLC(groups[thisgroup].Flast);
416 if (aa < 0.0)
417 {
418 /* in this case the group has
419 passed through the PLC since
420 last check, then compute its
421 time of PLC cross */
422 if ((Fplc = find_brent(groups[thisgroup].Flast, frag[iz].Fmax)) == -99.00)
423 return 1;
424 /* and store it */
425 if (store_PLC(Fplc))
426 return 1;
427 }
428 }
429 }
430 }
431 /* updates the Flast field */
432 groups[neigh[ig1]].Flast = frag[iz].Fmax;
433 }
434 /* restoring PBCs */
435 subbox.pbc[_x_] = storex;
436 subbox.pbc[_y_] = storey;
437 subbox.pbc[_z_] = storez;
438 }
439 else if (plc.Fstart > 0. && frag[iz].Fmax < plc.Fstart)
440 {
441 /* if nothing must be done, only update the Flast field */
442 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
443 groups[neigh[ig1]].Flast = frag[iz].Fmax;
444 }
445
446#endif
447 }
448 else
449 {
450 /* this closes the if (!skip) condition above: if the
451 particle is at the border of the domain it will not be a
452 peak and will have no neighbours */
453 peak_cond = 0;
454 neigrp = 0;
455 }
456
457 /* Is the point a peak? */
458 if (peak_cond)
459 {
460 /**********************************************************************
461 FIRST CASE: PEAK
462 **********************************************************************/
463
464 /* New group */
465 if (good_particle)
466 counters[0]++;
467
468 ngroups++;
469
470 /* this paranoid check will raise an error in case of major bugs */
471 if (ngroups > Npeaks + 2)
472 {
473 printf("OH MY DEAR, TASK %d FOUND TOO MANY GROUPS AT z=%f, STEP %d OF %d, THIS SHOULD NOT HAPPEN!\n", ThisTask, frag[iz].Fmax - 1.0, this_z, nstep);
474 return 1;
475 }
476
477 /* sets the properties of a one-particle group */
478 groups[ngroups].t_peak = frag[iz].Fmax;
479 groups[ngroups].t_appear = -1;
480 groups[ngroups].t_merge = -1;
481 groups[ngroups].Pos[0] = ibox + SHIFT;
482 groups[ngroups].Pos[1] = jbox + SHIFT;
483 groups[ngroups].Pos[2] = kbox + SHIFT;
484 groups[ngroups].Vel[0] = frag[iz].Vel[0];
485 groups[ngroups].Vel[1] = frag[iz].Vel[1];
486 groups[ngroups].Vel[2] = frag[iz].Vel[2];
487#ifdef TWO_LPT
488 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[0] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[0];
489 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[1] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[1];
490 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[2] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[2];
491#ifdef THREE_LPT
492 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[0];
493 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[1];
494 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[2];
495 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[0];
496 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[1];
497 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[2];
498#endif
499#endif
500#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
501 groups[ngroups].Vel_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_prev[0];
502 groups[ngroups].Vel_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_prev[1];
503 groups[ngroups].Vel_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_prev[2];
504#ifdef TWO_LPT
505 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT_prev[0];
506 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT_prev[1];
507 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT_prev[2];
508#ifdef THREE_LPT
509 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[0];
510 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[1];
511 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[2];
512 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[0];
513 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[1];
514 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[2];
515#endif
516#endif
517#endif
518 groups[ngroups].Mass = 1;
519 groups[ngroups].name = particle_name;
520 groups[ngroups].good = good_particle;
521 groups[ngroups].point = iz;
522 groups[ngroups].bottom = iz;
523 groups[ngroups].ll = ngroups;
524 groups[ngroups].halo_app = ngroups;
525#ifdef PLC
526 if (frag[iz].Fmax > plc.Fstart)
527 groups[ngroups].Flast = plc.Fstart;
528 else
529 groups[ngroups].Flast = frag[iz].Fmax;
530#endif
531
532 group_ID[iz] = ngroups;
533 linking_list[iz] = iz;
534 if (params.MinHaloMass == 1)
535 {
536 groups[ngroups].t_appear = frag[iz].Fmax;
537#ifdef SNAPSHOT
538 frag[iz].zacc = frag[iz].Fmax - 1;
539#endif
540 }
541 }
542 else if (neigrp == 1)
543 {
544
545 /**********************************************************************
546 SECOND CASE: 1 GROUP
547 **********************************************************************/
548
549 /* if the points touches only one group, check whether to accrete the point on it */
550
551 condition_for_accretion(1, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[0], &d2, &r2);
552
553 if (d2 < r2)
554 {
555
556 /*********************
557 accretion on a group!
558 *********************/
559
560 if (good_particle)
561 counters[7]++;
562 accrflag = 1;
563 to_group = neigh[0];
564 accretion(to_group, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
565 }
566 else
567 {
568 /*********
569 filament!
570 *********/
571
572 if (good_particle)
573 counters[12]++;
574 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
575 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
576 linking_list[iz] = iz;
577 }
578 }
579 else if (neigrp > 1)
580 {
581 /**********************************************************************
582 THIRD CASE: >1 GROUP
583 **********************************************************************/
584
585 /* In this case the point touches more than one group */
586
587 /*********************
588 accretion on a group?
589 *********************/
590
591 best_ratio = pow(10. * subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], 2.0);
592 accgrp = -1;
593 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
594 {
595 condition_for_accretion(2, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], &d2, &r2);
596 ratio = d2 / r2;
597 if (ratio < 1.0 && ratio < best_ratio)
598 {
599 best_ratio = ratio;
600 accgrp = ig1;
601 }
602 }
603
604 if (accgrp >= 0)
605 {
606 if (good_particle)
607 {
608 counters[7]++;
609 counters[8]++;
610 }
611 accrflag = 1;
612 to_group = neigh[accgrp];
613 accretion(neigh[accgrp], ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
614 }
615
616 /* Then checks by pairs whether the groups must be merged together */
617
618 nmerge = 0;
619 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
620 for (ig2 = 0; ig2 < ig1; ig2++)
621 {
622 merge[ig1][ig2] = 0;
623 condition_for_merging(frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], neigh[ig2], &merge_flag);
624 if (merge_flag)
625 {
626 merge[ig1][ig2] = 1;
627 nmerge++;
628 }
629 }
630
631 /******************
632 merging of groups!
633 ******************/
634
635 /* The group number of the largest group is preserved */
636
637 if (nmerge > 0)
638 {
639 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
640 for (ig2 = 0; ig2 < ig1; ig2++)
641 if (merge[ig1][ig2] == 1 && neigh[ig1] != neigh[ig2])
642 {
643 if (good_particle)
644 counters[10]++;
645 if (groups[neigh[ig1]].Mass > groups[neigh[ig2]].Mass)
646 {
647 merge_groups(neigh[ig1], neigh[ig2], frag[iz].Fmax);
648 large = neigh[ig1];
649 small = neigh[ig2];
650 }
651 else
652 {
653 merge_groups(neigh[ig2], neigh[ig1], frag[iz].Fmax);
654 small = neigh[ig1];
655 large = neigh[ig2];
656 }
657 if (to_group == small)
658 to_group = large;
659 for (ig3 = 0; ig3 < neigrp; ig3++)
660 if (neigh[ig3] == small)
661 neigh[ig3] = large;
662
663 if (groups[large].Mass < 5 * groups[small].Mass && good_particle)
664 counters[11]++;
665 }
666 }
667
668 /* If relevant, it tries again to accrete the particle */
669
670 if (accgrp == -1)
671 {
672 clean_list(neigh);
673
674 /* Number of neighbouring groups */
675 for (nn = neigrp = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
676 if (neigh[nn] > FILAMENT)
677 neigrp++;
678
679 best_ratio = pow(10. * subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], 2.0);
680 accgrp = -1;
681 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
682 {
683 condition_for_accretion(3, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], &d2, &r2);
684 ratio = d2 / r2;
685 if (ratio < best_ratio)
686 {
687 best_ratio = ratio;
688 accgrp = ig1;
689 }
690 }
691
692 if (best_ratio < 1)
693 {
694 if (good_particle)
695 {
696 counters[7]++;
697 counters[9]++;
698 }
699 accrflag = 1;
700 to_group = neigh[accgrp];
701 accretion(neigh[accgrp], ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
702 }
703 else
704 {
705 /* If the point has not been accreted at all: */
706
707 /*********
708 filament!
709 *********/
710
711 if (good_particle)
712 counters[12]++;
713 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
714 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
715 linking_list[iz] = iz;
716 }
717 }
718 }
719 else
720 {
721 /**********************************************************************
722 FOURTH CASE: FILAMENTS
723 **********************************************************************/
724 if (good_particle)
725 counters[12]++;
726 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
727 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
728 linking_list[iz] = iz;
729
730 /****************/
731 /* end of cases */
732 /****************/
733 }
734
735 /* If the particle was accreted somewhere, checks whether to
736 accrete to the same halo all the neighbouring filaments;
737 first it checks conditions for all filament particles, then
738 it accretes those that should */
739
740 if (accrflag && nf && !skip)
741 {
742 /* loop on filament particles */
743 for (ifil = 0; ifil < nf; ifil++)
744 {
745 condition_for_accretion(4, fil_list[ifil][0], fil_list[ifil][1], fil_list[ifil][2],
746 fil_list[ifil][3], frag[iz].Fmax, to_group, &d2, &r2);
747 /* tags filaments that should be accreted (the condition
748 should be checked without changing the halo) */
749 if (d2 < r2)
750 fil_list[ifil][3] *= -1;
751 }
752 /* accrete all filament particles */
753 for (ifil = 0; ifil < nf; ifil++)
754 if (fil_list[ifil][3] < 0)
755 {
756 fil_list[ifil][3] *= -1;
757 accretion(to_group, fil_list[ifil][0], fil_list[ifil][1],
758 fil_list[ifil][2], fil_list[ifil][3], frag[iz].Fmax);
759
760 groups[FILAMENT].Mass--;
761
762 if (fil_list[ifil][0] >= subbox.safe[_x_] &&
763 fil_list[ifil][0] < subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - subbox.safe[_x_] &&
764 fil_list[ifil][1] >= subbox.safe[_y_] &&
765 fil_list[ifil][1] < subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - subbox.safe[_y_] &&
766 fil_list[ifil][2] >= subbox.safe[_z_] &&
767 fil_list[ifil][2] < subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - subbox.safe[_z_])
768 {
769 counters[7]++;
770 counters[13]++;
771 counters[12]--;
772 }
773 }
774 }
775
776#ifdef PLC
777 /* if this is the end of the cycle for PLC, perform a last check
778 on all halos to see if some has passed through the PLC in
779 the meantime */
780 /* Note: this part is largely a replication of the PLC code above */
781 if (plc.Fstart > 0 && !last_check_done &&
782 (this_z == nstep - 1 || frag[iz].Fmax < plc.Fstop))
783 {
784
785 /* first write to disc what you have */
786 if (write_PLC(0))
787 return 1;
788 plc.Nstored = 0;
789
790 /* PBCs are switched off for this check */
791 storex = subbox.pbc[_x_];
792 storey = subbox.pbc[_y_];
793 storez = subbox.pbc[_z_];
794 subbox.pbc[_x_] = subbox.pbc[_y_] = subbox.pbc[_z_] = 0;
795
796 last_check_done = 1;
797 /* the check is performed on ALL groups */
798 for (ig1 = FILAMENT + 1; ig1 <= ngroups; ig1++)
799 {
800 /* is the group alive, good and massive enough? */
801 if (groups[ig1].point >= 0 && groups[ig1].good &&
802 groups[ig1].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
803 {
804 thisgroup = ig1;
805
806 /* loop on replications */
807 /* this loop may be threaded or ported to GPUs,
808 though it's relatively fast */
809 for (irep = 0; irep < plc.Nreplications; irep++)
810 /* checks that the redshift falls in the
811 range of the replication */
812 if (groups[ig1].Flast > plc.repls[irep].F2)
813 {
814 replicate[0] = plc.repls[irep].i;
815 replicate[1] = plc.repls[irep].j;
816 replicate[2] = plc.repls[irep].k;
817 /* this computes the difference between the
818 group distance from the observer and its
819 comoving distance at that z */
820 bb = condition_PLC(plc.Fstop);
821
822 /* in this unlikely case we catch the group
823just on the PLC */
824 if (bb == 0.0)
825 {
826 if (store_PLC(plc.Fstop))
827 return 1;
828 }
829 else if (bb > 0.)
830 {
831 /* if it is outside the PLC then check
832 whether it has just passed since
833 last check */
834 aa = condition_PLC(groups[ig1].Flast);
835 if (aa < 0.0)
836 {
837 /* in this case the group has passed
838through the PLC since last time */
839 if ((Fplc = find_brent(groups[ig1].Flast, plc.Fstop)) == -99.0)
840 return 1;
841 if (store_PLC(Fplc))
842 return 1;
843 }
844 }
845 }
846 }
847 }
848
849 /* restoring PBCs */
850 subbox.pbc[_x_] = storex;
851 subbox.pbc[_y_] = storey;
852 subbox.pbc[_z_] = storez;
853
854 if (!ThisTask)
855 printf("[%s] PLC: Last check on groups done, Task 0 stored %d halos (max:%d)\n",
856 fdate(), plc.Nstored, plc.Nmax);
857 cputime.plc += MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
858
859 if (write_PLC(1))
860 return 1;
861 }
862#endif
863
864 /************************************************************************
865 CLOSING THE CYCLE
866 ************************************************************************/
867
868 /* prints the fraction of steps done */
869 if (!ThisTask && !(this_z % nstep_p))
870 printf("[%s] *** %3d%% done, F = %6.2f, z = %6.2f\n", fdate(),
871 this_z / (nstep_p) * 5, frag[iz].Fmax,
872 frag[iz].Fmax - 1.0);
873
874 /* Write output if relevant.
875 The while cycle is here because it may happen that some
876 outputs are requested when there are very few collapsed
877 particles, and if no particle collapses between two catalogs
878 one catalog may not be written; better to have an empty
879 catalog, or two identical ones */
880 while (this_z == nstep - 1 || frag[iz].Fmax < outputs.F[iout])
881 {
882 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
883
884 if (!ThisTask)
885 printf("[%s] Writing output at z=%f\n", fdate(),
886 outputs.z[iout]);
887
888 fflush(stdout);
889 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
890
891 /* halo catalog */
892 if (write_catalog(iout))
893 return 1;
894
895 /* halo mass function */
896 if (compute_mf(iout))
897 return 1;
898
899 /* merger histories, only at the last redshift */
900 if (iout == outputs.n - 1)
901 {
902 if (write_histories())
903 return 1;
904 }
905
906 cputime.io += MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
907
908 /* if we are at the end, just break out of the while cycle */
909 iout++;
910 if (this_z == nstep - 1)
911 break;
912 }
913
914 if (this_z != nstep - 1 && frag[iz].Fmax < zstop + 1.0)
915 {
916 if (!ThisTask)
917 printf("[%s] Pausing fragmentation process\n", fdate());
918 last_z = this_z + 1;
919 return 0;
920 }
921
922 /************************************************************************
923 END OF DO-CYCLE ON COLLAPSED POINTS
924 ************************************************************************/
925 }
926
927 /* Counters */
928 for (ig1 = FILAMENT + 1; ig1 <= ngroups; ig1++)
929 if (groups[ig1].point >= 0 && groups[ig1].good)
930 counters[14]++;
931
932 MPI_Reduce(counters, all_counters, NCOUNTERS, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
933
934 /* statistics */
935 if (!ThisTask)
936 {
937 printf("Total number of peaks: %Lu\n", all_counters[0]);
938 printf("Total number of good halos: %Lu\n", all_counters[14]);
939 printf("Particles with N neighbouring groups: %Lu %Lu %Lu %Lu %Lu %Lu\n",
940 all_counters[1], all_counters[2], all_counters[3],
941 all_counters[4], all_counters[5], all_counters[6]);
942 printf("Total number of accretion events: %Lu\n", all_counters[7]);
943 printf("Accretion before evaluating merger: %Lu\n", all_counters[8]);
944 printf("Accretion after evaluating merger: %Lu\n", all_counters[9]);
945 printf("Accretion of filament particles: %Lu\n", all_counters[13]);
946 printf("\n");
947 printf("Global stats at the final redshift:\n");
948 printf("Total number of merger events: %Lu\n", all_counters[10]);
949 printf("Total number of major merger events: %Lu\n", all_counters[11]);
950 printf("Final number of filament particles: %Lu\n", all_counters[12]);
951 printf("Final number of particles in halos: %Lu\n", all_counters[0] + all_counters[7]);
952 printf("Total number of collapsed particles: %Lu\n", all_counters[0] + all_counters[7] + all_counters[12]);
953 printf("Total number of uncollapsed particles: %Lu\n", MyGrids[0].Ntotal - all_counters[0] - all_counters[7] - all_counters[12]);
954 printf("\n");
955 }
956
957#ifdef SNAPSHOT
958 /* Saving group_ID in the frag structure for the snapshot*/
959 groups[0].name = 0;
960 groups[FILAMENT].name = FILAMENT;
961 for (iz = 0; iz < subbox.Nstored; iz++)
962 frag[iz].group_ID = groups[group_ID[iz]].name;
963#endif
964
965#ifdef PLC
966
967 /* close the root solver */
968 gsl_root_fsolver_free(solver);
969
970#endif
971
972 /* Bye! */
973
974 return 0;
975}
NCOUNTERS
#define NCOUNTERS

447 /*----------------------------------------------------------------*/

449 /* Local average and variance declaration */
450 double local_average, local_variance;
451
452 /* Initialization */
453 local_variance = 0.0;
454 local_average = 0.0;
455
456 /* Initialize Fmax, Rmax and velocity if it is the first smoothing
457 The initialization is done in a parallel way
458 Each thread initializes its corresponding array elements
459 This can improve the initialization performance when executed on systems with multiple processors or cores */
460
461 if (!ismooth)
462
463 /* Loop on all particles */
464 #pragma omp parallel for
465 for (int i = 0; i < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; i++){
466
467 /*----------- Common initialization ----------- */
468 products[i].Fmax = -10.0;
469 products[i].Rmax = -1;
470
471 /*----------- Zel'dovich case ----------- */
472 products[i].Vel[0] = 0.0;
473 products[i].Vel[1] = 0.0;
474 products[i].Vel[2] = 0.0;
475
476#ifdef TWO_LPT
477
478 products[i].Vel_2LPT[0] = 0.0;
479 products[i].Vel_2LPT[1] = 0.0;
480 products[i].Vel_2LPT[2] = 0.0;
481
482#ifdef THREE_LPT
483
484 products[i].Vel_3LPT_1[0] = 0.0;
485 products[i].Vel_3LPT_1[1] = 0.0;
486 products[i].Vel_3LPT_1[2] = 0.0;
487 products[i].Vel_3LPT_2[0] = 0.0;
488 products[i].Vel_3LPT_2[1] = 0.0;
489 products[i].Vel_3LPT_2[2] = 0.0;
490#endif
491#endif
492 }
493
494 /* Declaration and initialization of variables when the openmp option is ON
495 Inside the parallel region, each thread initializes its own local variables i.e.
496 mylocal_average, mylocal_variance, cputime_invcoll, and cputime_ell
497 These variables are private to each thread and are not shared among threads, ensuring data consistency.*/
498
499#if defined( _OPENMP )
500
501 double invcoll_update = 0, ell_update = 0;
502
503 #pragma omp parallel
504 {
505
506 /* Thread-specific variables declaration */
507 double mylocal_average, mylocal_variance;
508 double cputime_invcoll;
509
510 /* Support variables */
511 int fails = 0;
512 int pid = omp_get_thread_num();
513
514 /* Initializitation */
515 mylocal_average = 0;
516 mylocal_variance = 0;
517 cputime_invcoll = 0;
518 cputime_ell = 0;
519
520#else
521
522 /* If openmp is OFF
523 This approach is used to avoid conditional compilation directives within the code and maintain consistency in variable
524 names across the parallel and serial versions of the code.
525 When OpenMP is disabled, the program uses the same variable names, but they refer to the original variables directly */
526
527#define mylocal_average local_average
528#define mylocal_variance local_variance
529#define cputime_invcoll cputime.invcoll
530#define cputime_ell cputime.ell
531
532#endif
533
534/*----------------- Calculation of variance, avarage and collapse time ----------------*/
535
536 double tmp = MPI_Wtime();
537
538/* When you use a parallel region, OpenMP will automatically wait for all threads to finish before execution */
539/* If you do not need synchronization after the loop, you can disable it with nowait */
540#ifdef _OPENMP
541#pragma omp for nowait
542#endif
543
544 /* Loop on all particle */
545 for (int index = 0; index < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; index++){
546
547 /* Computation of second derivatives of the potential i.e. the gravity Hessian */
548 double diff_ten[6];
549 for (int i = 0; i < 6; i++){
550 diff_ten[i] = second_derivatives[0][i][index];
551 }
552
553 /* Computation of the variance of the linear density field */
554 double delta = diff_ten[0] + diff_ten[1] + diff_ten[2];
555 mylocal_average += delta;
556 mylocal_variance += delta*delta;
557
558 /* Computation of the collapse time */
559 double lambda1,lambda2,lambda3;
560 int fail;
561 double Fnew = inverse_collapse_time(ismooth, diff_ten, &lambda1, &lambda2, &lambda3, &fail);
562
563 /*---------------------------DEBUG----------------------------------*/
564
565#ifdef DEBUG
566
567 // fprintf(collapseTimeFile, "%d %d %f %f %f %g\n", index, ismooth, lambda1, lambda2, lambda3, Fnew);
568
569#endif
570
571 /*-----------------------------------------------------------------*/
572
573 /* Fail check during computation of the collapse time */
574 if (fail){
575 printf("ERROR on task %d: failure in inverse_collapse_time\n",ThisTask);
576 fflush(stdout);
577
578/* Workflow control */
579#if !defined( _OPENMP )
580 return 1;
581#else
582 fails = 1;
583#endif
584 }
585
586 /* Updating collapse time */
587 if (products[index].Fmax < Fnew){
588 products[index].Fmax = Fnew;
589 products[index].Rmax = ismooth;
590 }
591 }
592
593 /* CPU collapse time */
594 cputime_invcoll += MPI_Wtime() - tmp;
595
596
597 /* ------------------- Updates local variance and local average using OpenMP atomic operations ------------------*/
598 /* local_variance and local_average are shared variables that are updated by each thread (mylocal_variance and mylocal_average)*/
599
600#if defined( _OPENMP )
601
602 #pragma omp atomic
603 local_variance += mylocal_variance;
604
605 #pragma omp atomic
606 local_average += mylocal_average;
607
608 /* ------------------------------------ Updates total CPU collapse time -----------------------------------*/
609 /* cputime_invcoll and cputime_ell are local variables that are updated by each thread and then accumulated using atomic operations */
610
611 #pragma omp atomic
612 invcoll_update += cputime_invcoll;
613
614 #pragma omp atomic
615 ell_update += cputime_ell;
616
617#endif
618
619#if defined( _OPENMP )
620 }
621#endif
622
623 int all_fails = 0;
624
625 /* -------------------------- Updates cpu collapse time for a single threads -----------------------------*/
626
627#if defined (_OPENMP)
628
629 #pragma omp atomic
630 cputime.invcoll += invcoll_update/internal.nthreads_omp;
631
632 #pragma omp atomic
633 cputime.ell += ell_update/internal.nthreads_omp;
634
635 /* fails is a local variable that indicates the number of failures in the computation.
636 Each thread updates fails, and then the values are accumulated using atomic operations */
637
638 #pragma omp atomic
639 all_fails += fails;
640
641#endif
642
643 /* Fail check during computation of the inverse collapse time */
644 /* If there were failures, an error message is printed and the function returns 1 */
645 if (all_fails){
646 printf("ERROR on task %d: failure in inverse_collapse_time\n",ThisTask);
647 fflush(stdout);
648 return 1;
649 }
650
651 /* Calculating obtained variance and avarage */
652 double global_variance = 0.0;
653 double global_average = 0.0;
654
655 /* Calculates the global variance and average by reducing the values of local_variance and local_average across all MPI tasks */
656 MPI_Reduce(&local_variance, &global_variance, 1, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
657 MPI_Reduce(&local_average , &global_average , 1, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
658
659 /* If the current task is the root task (task 0), it divides the global variance and average by the total number of data points (MyGrids[0].Ntotal)
660 to obtain the average values per data point */
661 if (!ThisTask){
662 global_variance /= (double)MyGrids[0].Ntotal;
663 global_average /= (double)MyGrids[0].Ntotal;
664 }
665
666 /* Broadcasts the value of global_variance from the root task to all other tasks using MPI_Bcast */
667 MPI_Bcast(&global_variance, 1, MPI_DOUBLE, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
668
669 /* Stores the true variance*/
670 Smoothing.TrueVariance[ismooth] = global_variance;
671
672 return 0;
673}

int compute_derivative ( int  ThisGrid,

Definition at line 173 of file fmax-fftw.c.

174{
175 int swap, local_x,local_y,local_z,ixx,iyy,izz,index,nxhalf,nyhalf,nzhalf;
176 double kx,ky,kz,kxnorm,kynorm,kznorm,green,k_squared,smoothing,tmp,time;
177 double diff_comp[4];
178
179
180#ifdef DEBUG
181 sprintf(filename,"results.%d-%d.%d",first_derivative,second_derivative,ThisTask);
182 results=fopen(filename,"w");
183#endif
184
185 /* k vectors */
186 kxnorm = 2.*PI/(double)MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_x;
187 kynorm = 2.*PI/(double)MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_y;
188 kznorm = 2.*PI/(double)MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_z;
189
190 /* Nyquist frequencies */
191 nxhalf = MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_x/2;
192 nyhalf = MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_y/2;
193 nzhalf = MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_z/2;
194
195 /* for first derivatives the real and imaginary parts must be swapped */
196 swap=((first_derivative==0 && second_derivative> 0) ||
197 (first_derivative> 0 && second_derivative==0));
198
199/*
200 NB: ix, iy and iz are global coordinates of the box (from 0 to N-1)
201 ix: [0,N/2]
202 iy, iz: [0,N-1]
203 iyy and izz are unfolded, they run from -N/2+1 to N/2 (ixx = ix)
204 local_x, local_y and local_z are local coordinates of the slice
205 (for the x and z coordinates they are the same as ix and iz)
206*/
207
208
209/* loop over k-space indices */
210/* This loop is correct for transposed order in FFTW */
211
212 for (local_z = 0; local_z < MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSlocal_k_z; local_z++)
213 {
214 izz = local_z + MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSstart_k_z;
215 if (local_z > nzhalf)
216 izz -= MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_z;
217 kz = kznorm*izz;
218
219 for (local_y = 0; local_y < MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSlocal_k_y; local_y++)
220 {
221 iyy = local_y + MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSstart_k_y;
222 if (iyy > nyhalf)
223 iyy -= MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_y;
224 ky = kynorm*iyy;
225
226 for (local_x = 0; local_x <= nxhalf; local_x++)
227 {
228 ixx = local_x;
229 kx = kxnorm*ixx;
230
231 k_squared = kx*kx + ky*ky + kz*kz;
232
233 /* corresponding index of real part in vector (imaginary in index + 1) */
234 index = 1 + 2*local_x + (MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_x+MyGrids[ThisGrid].off)
235 *(local_z + local_y* MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal_z);
236
237 if (k_squared != 0.)
238 {
239
240 /* Gaussian smoothing window */
241 smoothing = exp(-0.5 * k_squared * Rsmooth * Rsmooth);
242
243 /* the components are stored in the vectors that control the differentiation */
244 diff_comp[0] = 1.0;
245 diff_comp[1] = kx;
246 diff_comp[2] = ky;
247 diff_comp[3] = kz;
248
249 green = greens_function(diff_comp, k_squared, first_derivative, second_derivative);
250
251 (cvector_fft[ThisGrid][index/2])[0] *= green * smoothing;
252 (cvector_fft[ThisGrid][index/2])[1] *= green * smoothing;
253 }
254
255 if (swap)
256 {
257 tmp = (cvector_fft[ThisGrid][index/2])[1];
258 (cvector_fft[ThisGrid][index/2])[1] = (cvector_fft[ThisGrid][index/2])[0];
259 (cvector_fft[ThisGrid][index/2])[0] = -tmp;
260 }
261 }
262 }
263 }
264
265 if (!ThisTask)
266 printf("[%s] compute_derivative: starting fft\n",fdate());
267
268 time=reverse_transform(ThisGrid);
269
270 if (!ThisTask)
271 printf("[%s] compute_derivative: done fft, cpu time = %f\n",fdate(),time);
272
273 cputime.fft+=time;
274
275 return 0;
276}

36{

42 if (!ThisTask)
43 printf("[%s] First part: computation of collapse times\n", fdate());
44
45 ThisGrid = 0;
46
47 /*
48 TO EXTEND TO MULTIPLE GRIDS here we should
49
50 1) initialize fft for the large-scale grid (the same as the small-scale one?)
51 2) compute first derivatives of the large grid
52 3) compute second derivatives of the large grid
53 4) distribute to all processors and store the few used grid points
54 5) re-initialize fft for the small-scale grid
55 */
56
57 ScaleDep.order = 0; /* collapse times must be computed as for LambdaCDM */
58 ScaleDep.redshift = 0.0;
59
60 /****************************
61 * CYCLE ON SMOOTHING RADII *
62 ****************************/
63
64 for (ismooth = 0; ismooth < Smoothing.Nsmooth; ismooth++)
65 {
66 if (!ThisTask)
67 printf("\n[%s] Starting smoothing radius %d of %d (R=%9.5f, sigma=%9.5f)\n",
68 fdate(), ismooth + 1, Smoothing.Nsmooth, Smoothing.Radius[ismooth],
69 sqrt(Smoothing.Variance[ismooth]));
70
71 cpusm = MPI_Wtime();
72
73 /*
74 Part 1:
75 Compute second derivatives of the potential
76 */
77
78 if (!ThisTask)
79 printf("[%s] Computing second derivatives\n", fdate());
80
81 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
82
83 if (compute_second_derivatives(Smoothing.Radius[ismooth], ThisGrid))
84 return 1;
85
86 cputmp = MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
87 if (!ThisTask)
88 printf("[%s] Done second derivatives, cpu time = %f s\n", fdate(), cputmp);
89 cputime.deriv += cputmp;
90
91 /*
92 Part 2:
93 Compute collapse times
94 */
95
96 if (!ThisTask)
97 printf("[%s] Computing collapse times\n", fdate());
98
99 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
100
101#ifdef TABULATED_CT
102 /* initialize spline for interpolating collapse times */
103 if (initialize_collapse_times(ismooth, 0))
104 return 1;
105
106 cputmp = MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
107
108 if (!ThisTask)
109 {
110 if (strcmp(params.CTtableFile, "none"))
111 printf("[%s] Collapse times read from file %s\n", fdate(), params.CTtableFile);
112 else
113 printf("[%s] Collapse times computed for interpolation, cpu time =%f s\n", fdate(), cputmp);
114 }
115
116 cputime.coll += cputmp;
117 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
118#endif
119
120 if (compute_collapse_times(ismooth))
121 return 1;
122
123#ifdef TABULATED_CT
124 /* this is needed only for debug options, to be removed in the official code */
125 if (reset_collapse_times(ismooth))
126 return 1;
127#endif
128
129 cputmp = MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
130 if (!ThisTask)
131 printf("[%s] Done computing collapse times, cpu time = %f s\n", fdate(), cputmp);
132 cputime.coll += cputmp;
133
134 /*
135End of cycle on smoothing radii
136 */
137
138 cpusm = MPI_Wtime() - cpusm;
139
140 if (!ThisTask)
141 printf("[%s] Completed, R=%6.3f, expected sigma: %7.4f, computed sigma: %7.4f, cpu time = %f s\n", fdate(),
142 Smoothing.Radius[ismooth], sqrt(Smoothing.Variance[ismooth]),
143 sqrt(Smoothing.TrueVariance[ismooth]),
144 cpusm);
145
146 fflush(stdout);
147 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
148 }
149
150 /********************************
151 * COMPUTATION OF DISPLACEMENTS *
152 ********************************/
153
154 /* computes the displacements for all particles at zero smoothing,
155 assuming second derivatives are already in place
156 displacements are computed to the redshift of the first (or only) segment
157 */
158 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
159 if (!ThisTask)
160 printf("\n[%s] Computing displacements for redshift %f\n", fdate(), ScaleDep.z[0]);
161
162 if (compute_displacements(1, 0, ScaleDep.z[0]))
163 return 1;
164
165 cputmp = MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
166 if (!ThisTask)
167 printf("[%s] Done computing displacements, cpu time = %f s\n", fdate(), cputmp);
168
169 /***************/
170 /* END OF FMAX */
171 /***************/
172
173 if (finalize_fft())
174 return 1;
175
176 if (Fmax_PDF())
177 return 1;
178
179 cputime.fmax = MPI_Wtime() - cputime.fmax;
180 if (!ThisTask)
181 printf("[%s] Finishing fmax, total fmax cpu time = %14.6f\n"
182 "\t\t IO : %14.6f (%14.6f total time without I/O)\n"
183 "\t\t FFT : %14.6f\n"
184 "\t\t COLLAPSE : %14.6f\n",
185 fdate(), cputime.fmax, cputime.io, cputime.fmax - cputime.io, cputime.fft, cputime.coll);
186
187 return 0;
188}

33{
34 double time;
35
36 /*
37 second derivative components are sorted this way
38 0 -> (1,1)
39 1 -> (2,2)
40 2 -> (3,3)
41 3 -> (1,2)
42 4 -> (1,3)
43 5 -> (2,3)
44 */
45
46 if (compute_sources)
47 {
48
49 /**************************/
50 /* Computation of sources */
51 /**************************/
52
53 ScaleDep.order = 0; /* sources are computed as for LambdaCDM */
54 ScaleDep.redshift = 0.0;
55
56 /* Computes the source for 2 LPT, 3LPT_1 and 3LPT_2 term */
57 /* loop on all local particles */
58#ifdef _OPENMP
59#pragma omp parallel
60 {
61
62#pragma omp for nowait
63#endif
64 for (int index = 0; index < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; index++)
65 {
66
67 /* HERE IT WILL BE NECESSARY TO SUM DIFFERENT SOURCES IF MULTIPLE GRIDS ARE USED */
68
69 /* source term for 2LPT */
70 source_2LPT[index] =
71 second_derivatives[0][0][index] * second_derivatives[0][1][index] +
72 second_derivatives[0][0][index] * second_derivatives[0][2][index] +
73 second_derivatives[0][1][index] * second_derivatives[0][2][index] -
74 second_derivatives[0][3][index] * second_derivatives[0][3][index] -
75 second_derivatives[0][4][index] * second_derivatives[0][4][index] -
76 second_derivatives[0][5][index] * second_derivatives[0][5][index];
77
78#ifdef THREE_LPT
79 source_3LPT_1[index] = 3.0 * (second_derivatives[0][0][index] *
80 (second_derivatives[0][1][index] * second_derivatives[0][2][index] - second_derivatives[0][5][index] * second_derivatives[0][5][index]) -
81 second_derivatives[0][3][index] *
82 (second_derivatives[0][3][index] * second_derivatives[0][2][index] - second_derivatives[0][4][index] * second_derivatives[0][5][index]) +
83 second_derivatives[0][4][index] *
84 (second_derivatives[0][3][index] * second_derivatives[0][5][index] - second_derivatives[0][4][index] * second_derivatives[0][1][index]));
85
86 source_3LPT_2[index] = 2.0 * /* this factor is needed because nabla2phi is half the theoretical one */
87 (second_derivatives[0][0][index] + second_derivatives[0][1][index] + second_derivatives[0][2][index]) *
88 source_2LPT[index];
89#endif
90 }
91#ifdef _OPENMP
92 }
93#endif
94
95 /* forward FFT for 2LPT source */
96 write_in_rvector(0, source_2LPT);
97
98 if (!ThisTask)
99 printf("[%s] source for 2LPT: starting fft\n", fdate());
100
101 time = forward_transform(0);
102
103 if (!ThisTask)
104 printf("[%s] source for 2LPT: done fft, cputime = %f\n", fdate(), time);
105
106 cputime.fft += time;
107
108 write_from_cvector(0, kvector_2LPT);
109
110#ifdef THREE_LPT
111
112 /* second derivatives of second-order potential */
113 for (int ia = 1; ia <= 3; ia++)
114 for (int ib = ia; ib <= 3; ib++)
115 {
116 int ider = (ia == ib ? ia : ia + ib + 1);
117
118 if (!ThisTask)
119 printf("[%s] Computing 2nd derivative of 2LPT source: %d\n", fdate(), ia);
120
121 write_in_cvector(0, kvector_2LPT);
122
123 if (compute_derivative(0, ia, ib))
124 return 1;
125
126#ifdef _OPENMP
127#pragma omp parallel
128 {
129#pragma omp for nowait
130#endif
131 for (int index = 0; index < MyGrids[0].total_local_size; index++)
132 /* the first 2 factor is needed because nabla2phi is half the theoretical one */
133 source_3LPT_2[index] -= 2.0 * (ider <= 3 ? 1.0 : 2.0) *
134 rvector_fft[0][index] * second_derivatives[0][ider - 1][index];
135#ifdef _OPENMP
136 }
137#endif
138 }
139
140 /* forward FFT for 3LPT_1 source */
141 write_in_rvector(0, source_3LPT_1);
142
143 if (!ThisTask)
144 printf("[%s] source for 3LPT: starting fft\n", fdate());
145
146 time = forward_transform(0);
147
148 if (!ThisTask)
149 printf("[%s] sources for 3LPT: done fft, cputime = %f\n", fdate(), time);
150
151 cputime.fft += time;
152
153 write_from_cvector(0, kvector_3LPT_1);
154
155 /* forward FFT for 3LPT_2 source */
156 write_in_rvector(0, source_3LPT_2);
157
158 if (!ThisTask)
159 printf("[%s] source for 3LPT: starting fft\n", fdate());
160
161 time = forward_transform(0);
162
163 if (!ThisTask)
164 printf("[%s] source for 3LPT: done fft, cputime = %f\n", fdate(), time);
165
166 cputime.fft += time;
167
168 write_from_cvector(0, kvector_3LPT_2);
169
170#endif
171 }
172
173 /* displacements for the three terms */
174 if (!ThisTask)
175 printf("[%s] Computing displacements\n", fdate());
176
177 ScaleDep.order = 2; /* here we need the 2LPT */
178 ScaleDep.redshift = redshift;
179
180 compute_first_derivatives(0., 0, 2, kvector_2LPT);
181
182 /* for ( int ia = 1; ia <= 3; ia++ ) */

50 mf.massinbin_local[i] = 0.0;
51 mf.ninbin[i] = 0;
52 mf.massinbin[i] = 0.0;
53 }
54
55 /* constructs the histograms */
56 for (i = FILAMENT + 1; i <= ngroups; i++)
57 if (groups[i].point >= 0 && groups[i].good && groups[i].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
58 {
59 amass = groups[i].Mass * params.ParticleMass;
60 ibin = (int)((log10(amass) - mf.mmin) / DELTAM);
61 if (ibin < 0 || ibin >= mf.NBIN)
62 continue;
63 mf.ninbin_local[ibin]++;
64 mf.massinbin_local[ibin] += amass;
65 }
66
67 /* sums counters over all tasks */
68 MPI_Reduce(mf.ninbin_local, mf.ninbin, mf.NBIN, MPI_INT, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
69 MPI_Reduce(mf.massinbin_local, mf.massinbin, mf.NBIN, MPI_DOUBLE, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
70
71 /* Writes result */
72 if (!ThisTask)
73 {
74 sprintf(filename, "pinocchio.%6.4f.%s.mf.out", outputs.z[iout], params.RunFlag);
75 printf("[%s] Writing mass function into file %s\n", fdate(), filename);
76 file = fopen(filename, "w");
77
78 fprintf(file, "# Mass function for redshift %f\n", outputs.z[iout]);
79 if (params.OutputInH100)
80 {
81 strcpy(lab1, "/h");
82 strcpy(lab2, "h^4");
83 }
84 else
85 {
86 strcpy(lab1, "");
87 strcpy(lab2, "");
88 }
89
90 fprintf(file, "# 1) mass (Msun%s)\n", lab1);
91 fprintf(file, "# 2) n(m) (Mpc^-3 Msun^-1 %s)\n", lab2);
92 fprintf(file, "# 3) upper +1-sigma limit for n(m) (Mpc^-3 Msun^-1 %s)\n", lab2);
93 fprintf(file, "# 4) lower -1-sigma limit for n(m) (Mpc^-3 Msun^-1 %s)\n", lab2);
94 fprintf(file, "# 5) number of halos in the bin\n");
95 switch (params.AnalyticMassFunction)
96 {
97 case 0:
98 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Press & Schechter 1974\n");
99 break;
100 case 1:
101 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Sheth & Tormen 2001\n");
102 break;
103 case 2:
104 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Jenkins et al. 2001\n");
105 break;
106 case 3:
107 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Warren et al. 2006\n");
108 break;
109 case 4:
110 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Reed et al. 2007\n");
111 break;
112 case 5:
113 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Crocce et al. 2010\n");
114 break;
115 case 6:
116 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Tinker et al. 2010\n");
117 break;
118 case 7:
119 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Courtin et al. 2010\n");
120 break;
121 case 8:
122 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Angulo et al. 2012\n");
123 break;
124 case 9:
125 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Watson et al. 2013\n");
126 break;
127 case 10:
128 fprintf(file, "# 6) analytical n(m) from Crocce et al. 2010, universal\n");
129 break;
130 default:
131 break;
132 }
133 fprintf(file, "#\n");
134
135 for (i = 0; i < mf.NBIN; i++)
136 {
137 x = mf.mmin + (i + 0.5) * DELTAM;
138 m = pow(10., x);
139 dm = params.ParticleMass *
140 (double)((int)(pow(10., mf.mmin + (i + 1) * DELTAM) / params.ParticleMass) -
141 (int)(pow(10., mf.mmin + i * DELTAM) / params.ParticleMass));
142 if (dm > 0.0)
143 {
144 a = (double)mf.ninbin[i] / mf.vol / dm;
145 a1 = ((double)mf.ninbin[i] + sqrt((double)mf.ninbin[i])) / mf.vol / dm;
146 a2 = ((double)mf.ninbin[i] - sqrt((double)mf.ninbin[i])) / mf.vol / dm;
147 }
148 else
149 a = a1 = a2 = 0.0;
150
151 if (mf.ninbin[i] > 1)
152 mx = mf.massinbin[i] / (double)mf.ninbin[i];
153 else
154 mx = m;
155 a3 = AnalyticMassFunction(mx, outputs.z[iout]);
156
157 r = SizeForMass(mx);
158 massvar = MassVariance(r) * D * D;
159 sigma = sqrt(massvar);
160 ni = 1.686 / sigma;
161
162 fprintf(file,
163 " %15.8g %15.8g %15.8g %15.8g %10d %15.8g %15.8g\n",
164 mx * mf.hfactor,
165 a / mf.hfactor4,
166 a1 / mf.hfactor4,
167 a2 / mf.hfactor4,
168 mf.ninbin[i],
169 a3 / mf.hfactor4,
170 ni);
171 }
172 fclose(file);
173 }
174
175 // LEVARE
176 /* if (iout==1) */
177 /* { */
178 /* FILE *ff=fopen("mydump","w"); */
179 /* for (int u=0; u<subbox.Nstored; u++) */
180 /* #ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS */
181 /* fprintf(ff," %d %d %d %f %f %f\n", */
182 /* u,frag_pos[u],sorted_pos[u],frag[u].Fmax,frag[u].Vel[0],frag[u].Vel_prev[0]); */
183 /* #else */
184 /* fprintf(ff," %d %d %d %f %f %f\n", */
185 /* u,frag_pos[u],sorted_pos[u],frag[u].Fmax,frag[u].Vel[0],0.0); */
186 /* #endif */
187 /* fclose(ff); */
188 /* } */
189
190 /* Bye! */
191 return 0;
192}

double compute_Nhalos_in_PLC ( double  z1,

)

Definition at line 935 of file fragment.c.

936{
937 /* analytic prediction of the number of halos in the PLC */
938
939 double MinMass = log(params.ParticleMass * params.MinHaloMass);
940 double delta_z = 0.01;
941 double number = 0;
942 double solidangle = (1 - cos((params.PLCAperture > 90. ? 90. : params.PLCAperture) / 180. * PI)) * 2. * PI;
943
944 double result, error, upper, lower;
945 gsl_function Function;
946
947 Function.function = &Integrand_MF;
948 lower = z1;
949 do
950 {
951 upper = lower + delta_z;
952 if (upper > z2)
953 upper = z2;
954 myz = 0.5 * (upper + lower);
955
956 gsl_integration_qags(&Function, MinMass, 37.0, 0.0, TOLERANCE, NWINT, workspace, &result, &error);
957
958 number += result * solidangle * (pow(ComovingDistance(upper), 3.) - pow(ComovingDistance(lower), 3.)) / 3.;
959 lower += delta_z;
960 } while (upper < z2);
961
962 return number;
963}

1268 /* Checks whether a particle should be accreted on a group */

1275
1276 set_point(i, j, k, ind, Fmax, &obj1);
1277 set_obj(grp, Fmax, &obj2);
1278

1281 /* to shorten the calculation, distances are checked dim by dim */

1285 {

59{
60 /* Distributes products from fft-space to sub-volumes */
61
62 int my_fft_box[6],my_subbox[6];
63 int log_ntask, bit, receiver, sender;
64
65 /* this defines the box that the task possesses in the FFT space */
66 my_fft_box[0] = MyGrids[0].GSstart[_x_];
67 my_fft_box[1] = MyGrids[0].GSstart[_y_];
68 my_fft_box[2] = MyGrids[0].GSstart[_z_];
69 my_fft_box[3] = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_x_];
70 my_fft_box[4] = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_y_];
71 my_fft_box[5] = MyGrids[0].GSlocal[_z_];
72
73 /* this defines the box that the task possesses in the subbox space
74 (the starting coordinate may be negative) */
75 my_subbox[0] = subbox.stabl[_x_];
76 my_subbox[1] = subbox.stabl[_y_];
77 my_subbox[2] = subbox.stabl[_z_];
78 my_subbox[3] = subbox.Lgwbl[_x_];

83 char fname[SBLENGTH];
84 sprintf(fname,"Task%d.dbg",ThisTask);
85 DBGFD = fopen(fname,"a");
86#endif
87
88 /* let's synchronize the tasks here */
89 fflush(stdout);
90 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
91
92 /* these are the already stored particles */
93#ifndef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
94 frag_offset=subbox.Nneeded;
95#endif
96
97 /* stores the data relative to the intersection of the fft box and subbox */
98 if (keep_data(my_fft_box, my_subbox))
99 return 1;
100
101 comm_buffer=(product_data*)calloc(BUFLEN , sizeof(product_data));
102 if (comm_buffer==0x0)
103 {
104 printf("ERROR on task %d: could not allocate comm_buffer in distribute\n",ThisTask);
105 fflush (stdout);
106 return 1;
107 }
108
109 /* hypercubic communication scheme */
110 for (log_ntask=0; log_ntask<1000; log_ntask++)
111 if (1<<log_ntask >= NTasks)
112 break;
113
114 /* loop on hypercube dimension */
115 for (bit=1; bit<1<<log_ntask; bit++)
116 {
117 /* loop on tasks */
118 for (sender=0; sender<NTasks; sender++)
119 {
120 /* receiver task is computed with a bitwise xor */
121 receiver = sender ^ bit;
122
123 /* condition on sender and receiver */
124 if (receiver < NTasks && sender < receiver)
125 {
126
127 /* the communication will be done
128 first sender -> receiver and then receiver -> sender */
129
130 if (ThisTask==sender)
131 send_data(my_fft_box, receiver);
132 else if (ThisTask==receiver)
133 {
134 if (recv_data(my_subbox, sender))
135 return 1;
136 }
137
138 if (ThisTask==receiver)
139 send_data(my_fft_box, sender);
140 else if (ThisTask==sender)
141 {
142 if (recv_data(my_subbox, receiver))
143 return 1;
144 }
145
146 }
147 }
148 }
149
150
151 /* updates the number of stored particles */
152#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
153 subbox.Nstored=subbox.Npart;
154#else
155
156 if (frag_offset > subbox.Nalloc)
157 subbox.Nstored=subbox.Nalloc;
158 else
159 subbox.Nstored=frag_offset;
160
161 subbox.Nneeded=frag_offset;
162
163#endif
164
165 free(comm_buffer);
166
167 fflush(stdout);
168 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
169
170#ifdef DEBUG
171 fclose(DBGFD);
172#endif
173
174 return 0;
175}

765 {
766
767 /* the communication will be done
768 first sender -> receiver and then receiver -> sender */
769
770 if (ThisTask==sender)
771 send_data_back(receiver);
772 else if (ThisTask==receiver)
773 {
774 if (recv_data_back(my_fft_box, sender))
775 return 1;
776 }
777
778 if (ThisTask==receiver)
779 send_data_back(sender);
780 else if (ThisTask==sender)
781 {
782 if (recv_data_back(my_fft_box, receiver))
783 return 1;
784 }
785
786 }
787 }
788 }
789
790
791 free(back_buffer);
792
793 fflush(stdout);
794 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
795
796 return 0;
797}

2138{
2139
2140 /*
2141 dOmega/dLambda
2142
2143 DIMENSIONLESS
2144
2145 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 0: Press & Schechter (1974)
2146 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 1: Sheth & Tormen (2001)
2147 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 2: Jenkins et al. (2001)
2148 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 3: Warren et al. (2006)
2149 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 4: Reed et al. (2007)
2150 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 5: Crocce et al. (2010)
2151 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 6: Tinker et al. (2008)
2152 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 7: Courtin et al. (2010)
2153 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 8: Angulo et al. (2012)
2154 params.AnalyticMassFunction = 9: Watson et al. (2013)
2155 params.AnalyticMassFunction =10: Crocce et al. (2010), with forced universality
2156
2157 */
2158
2159 double sv, ni, ni2, onepz;
2160
2161 sv = sqrt(v);
2162 ni = DELTA_C / sv;
2163
2164 switch (params.AnalyticMassFunction)
2165 {
2166 case 0: // Press & Schechter
2167 return 2. * exp(-0.5 * ni * ni) * ni * SQRT2PI;
2168 break;
2169
2170 case 1: // Sheth & Tormen
2171 ni2 = sqrt(0.707) * ni;
2172 return 2. * 0.3222 * SQRT2PI * ni2 * exp(-0.5 * ni2 * ni2) * (1.0 + 1.0 / pow(ni2, 0.6));
2173 break;
2174
2175 case 2: // Jenkins et al.
2176 return 0.315 * exp(-pow(fabs(-log(sv) + 0.61), 3.8));
2177 break;
2178
2179 case 3: // Warren et al. (2006)
2180 return 0.7234 * (pow(sv, -1.625) + 0.2538) * exp(-1.1982 / v);
2181 break;
2182
2183 case 4: // Reed et al. (2007)
2184 ni2 = sqrt(0.707) * ni;
2185 return 2. * 0.3222 * SQRT2PI * ni2 * exp(-0.54 * ni2 * ni2) *
2186 (1.0 + 1.0 / pow(ni2, 0.6) + 0.2 * exp(-(pow(-log(sv) - 0.4, 2.0) / 0.72)));
2187 break;
2188
2189 case 5: // Crocce et al. (2010)
2190 onepz = (z < 1 ? 1.0 + z : 2.0);
2191 return 0.58 * pow(onepz, -0.13) * (pow(sv, -1.37 * pow(onepz, -0.15)) + 0.3 * pow(onepz, -0.084)) * exp(-1.036 * pow(onepz, -0.024) / v);
2192 break;
2193
2194 case 6: // Tinker et al. (2010)
2195 onepz = (z < 2.5 ? 1.0 + z : 3.5);
2196 return 0.186 * pow(onepz, -0.14) * (pow(2.57 * pow(onepz, -0.569558118758974) / sv, 1.47 * pow(onepz, -0.06)) + 1.) * exp(-1.19 / v);
2197 break;
2198
2199 case 7: // Courtin et al. (2010)
2200 ni2 = sqrt(0.695) * 1.673 / sv;
2201 return 0.348 * 2. * SQRT2PI * ni2 * (1. + pow(1. / ni2 / ni2, 0.1)) * exp(-ni2 * ni2 / 2.);
2202 break;
2203
2204 case 8: // Angulo et al. (2012)
2205 return 0.201 * (pow(ni * 2.08 / DELTA_C, 1.7) + 1.0) * exp(-1.172 * ni * ni / DELTA_C / DELTA_C);
2206 break;
2207
2208 case 9: // Watson et al. (2013)
2209 return 0.282 * (pow(ni * 1.406 / DELTA_C, 2.163) + 1.0) * exp(-1.210 * ni * ni / DELTA_C / DELTA_C);
2210 break;
2211
2212 case 10: // Crocce et al. (2010) universal
2213 onepz = 1.0;
2214 return 0.58 * pow(onepz, -0.13) * (pow(sv, -1.37 * pow(onepz, -0.15)) + 0.3 * pow(onepz, -0.084)) * exp(-1.036 * pow(onepz, -0.024) / v);
2215 break;
2216
2217 default:
2218 return 0.0;
2219 break;
2220 }
2221}

420{
421 /* dumps fmax products on files to reuse them */
422
423 struct stat dr;
424 FILE *file;
425 char fname[LBLENGTH];
426
427 /* Task 0 checks that the DumpProducts directory exists, or creates it
428 and writes a summary file to check that one does not start from a different run */
429 if (!ThisTask)
430 {
431 if (stat(params.DumpDir, &dr))
432 {
433 printf("Creating directory %s\n", params.DumpDir);
434 if (mkdir(params.DumpDir, 0755))
435 {
436 printf("ERROR IN CREATING DIRECTORY %s (task 0)\n", params.DumpDir);
437 return 1;
438 }
439 }
440
441 sprintf(fname, "%ssummary", params.DumpDir);
442 file = fopen(fname, "w");
443 fprintf(file, "%d # NTasks\n", NTasks);
444 fprintf(file, "%d # random seed\n", params.RandomSeed);
445 fprintf(file, "%d # grid size\n", params.GridSize[0]);
446 fprintf(file, "%d # length of product_data\n", (int)sizeof(product_data));
447 fclose(file);
448
449 /* dumps the true variances */
450 sprintf(fname, "%sTrueVariance", params.DumpDir);
451 file = fopen(fname, "wb");
452 fwrite(Smoothing.TrueVariance, sizeof(double), Smoothing.Nsmooth, file);
453 fclose(file);
454 }
455
456 /* all tasks must wait for Task 0 to open the directory (if needed) */
457 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
458
459 /* each task writes a separate dump file */
460 sprintf(fname, "%sTask.%d", params.DumpDir, ThisTask);
461 file = fopen(fname, "wb");
462 if (file == 0x0)
463 {
464 printf("ERROR on Task %d: could not open file %s\n", ThisTask, fname);
465 return 1;
466 }
467
468 // NOTE: with LEAPFROG one needs to swap also kdensity and kvectors
469 fwrite(products, sizeof(product_data), MyGrids[0].total_local_size, file);
470 fclose(file);
471
472 return 0;
473}

91 GRID.GSstart_k[2], GRID.GSstart_k[2] + GRID.GSlocal_k[2]);
92
93
94 // -----------------------------------------------
95 // generate seed plane
96 // bottom-left and top-right corners for the region of k-plane
97 // that this Task must deal with
98
99
100 // prepare coordinates
101 if ( !params.use_transposed_fft )
102 // this is the non-transposed order x, y, z
103 for(int i = 0; i < 3; i++)
104 C[i] = i;
105 else
106 {
107 if ( internal.tasks_subdivision_dim > 1 )
108 C[0] = _y_, C[1] = _z_, C[2] = _x_;
109 else
110 C[0] = _y_, C[1] = _x_, C[2] = _z_;
111 }
112
113 // set up starting points and lenghts
114 for(int i = 0; i< 3; i++)
115 {
116 local_n [i] = GRID.GSlocal_k[i];
117 local_start[i] = GRID.GSstart_k[i];
118 }
119
120 // actually generate seeds
121 {
122 map_point bottom_left, top_right;
123 map_point plane_bottom_left, plane_top_right;
124
125 bottom_left[_x_] = GRID.GSstart_k[_x_];
126 bottom_left[_y_] = GRID.GSstart_k[_y_];
127
128 top_right[_x_] = GRID.GSstart_k[_x_] + GRID.GSlocal_k[_x_];
129 top_right[_y_] = GRID.GSstart_k[_y_] + GRID.GSlocal_k[_y_];
130
131 if( !internal.large_plane )
132 {
133 plane_bottom_left[_x_] = 0;
134 plane_bottom_left[_y_] = 0;
135
136 plane_top_right[_x_] = Nmesh;
137 plane_top_right[_y_] = Nmesh;
138 }
139 else
140 {
141 plane_bottom_left[_x_] = bottom_left[_x_];
142 plane_bottom_left[_y_] = bottom_left[_y_];
143 plane_top_right[_x_] = top_right[_x_];
144 plane_top_right[_y_] = top_right[_y_];
145 }
146
147 int ret = generate_seeds_plane(ThisGrid, plane_bottom_left, plane_top_right, Nmesh, (local_start[_z_] == 0));
148
149 if( ret > 0 )
150 return ret;
151
152 if ( internal.dump_seedplane )
153 {
154
155 if ( params.use_transposed_fft && internal.tasks_subdivision_dim > 1 )
156 C[1] = _x_;
157 dump_seed_plane(ThisGrid, bottom_left, top_right, C, local_n[_x_], GRID.GSstart_k[_z_], Nmesh);
158 }
159 }
160
161 // -----------------------------------------------
162
163
164
165
166 // the vector is initialized to zero
167 memset( kdensity[ThisGrid], 0, VectorLength * sizeof(double) );
168
169 int Nmesh_2, Nmesh_odd;
170 gsl_rng *k0_generator;
171
172 Nmesh_2 = Nmesh / 2;
173 Nmesh_odd = (Nmesh % 2);
174
175
176 // initialize the pseudo-random number chain
177
178 gsl_rng_set(random_generator, params.RandomSeed);
179
180 // initialize a second random generator, used on the plane k = 0
181
182 k0_generator = gsl_rng_alloc(gsl_rng_ranlxd1);
183
184 // --------------------------------------------------------------
185 // this is the main loop on grid modes
186 //--------------------------------------------------------------
187
188 for( int i = 0; i < local_n[_x_]; i++ )
189 {
190
191 // ii is the x grid coordinate
192 int ii = local_start[_x_] + i;
193 if ( ii == Nmesh_2 )
194 continue;
195
196 double kvec[3];
197
198 if ( ii < Nmesh_2 )
199 kvec[0] = ii * 2 * PI / Box;
200 else
201 kvec[0] = -(Nmesh - ii) * 2 * PI / Box;
202
203 double kmag2_i = kvec[0] * kvec[0];
204 int iii = ii;
205
206
207 for(int j = 0; j < local_n[_y_]; j++)
208 {
209
210 // jj is the y grid coordinate
211 int jj = local_start[_y_] + j;
212 if(jj == Nmesh_2)
213 continue;
214 int jjj = jj;
215
216 if(jj < Nmesh_2)
217 kvec[1] = jj * 2 * PI / Box;
218 else
219 kvec[1] = -(Nmesh - jj) * 2 * PI / Box;
220
221 double kmag2_ij = kmag2_i + kvec[1] * kvec[1];
222
223 unsigned int seed;
224
225 // initialize the random generator with the seed found in the
226 // seedtable at the (i,j) coordinate in grid coordinates
227 if(internal.large_plane)
228 {
229 gsl_rng_set(random_generator, SEEDTABLE[j * local_n[_x_] + i]);
230 seed = SEEDTABLE[j * local_n[_x_] + i];
231 }
232 else
233 {
234 gsl_rng_set(random_generator, SEEDTABLE[jj * Nmesh + ii]);
235 seed = SEEDTABLE[jj * Nmesh + ii];
236 }
237
238 // since the chain of random numbers is subsequent, to
239 // reproduce all the time the same sequence, this
240 // process must generate all the random numbers on the
241 // k column that are below its starting k coordinate
242
243 double phase;
244 if(local_start[_z_] > 0 && local_start[_z_] < Nmesh_2)
245 for(int RR = 0; RR < local_start[_z_]; RR++)
246 {
247 phase = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator);
248 do
249 phase = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator);
250 while(phase == 0);
251 }
252
253 // inner loop
254 for(int k = 0; (k < local_n[_z_]) && (k+local_start[_z_] < Nmesh_2); k++)
255 {
256 // kk is the z grid coordinate
257 int kk = local_start[_z_] + k;
258
259 // generate phase and amplitude
260 phase = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator) * 2 * PI;
261 double ampl;
262 do
263 ampl = gsl_rng_uniform(random_generator);
264 while(ampl == 0);
265
266 // blind points on the cube
267 if(ii == 0 && jj == 0 && kk == 0)
268 continue;
269 if(kk == Nmesh_2)
270 continue;
271
272 if(kk < Nmesh_2)
273 kvec[2] = kk * 2 * PI / Box;
274 else
275 kvec[2] = -(Nmesh - kk) * 2 * PI / Box;
276
277 double kmag2_local = kmag2_ij + kvec[2] * kvec[2];
278 double kmag = sqrt(kmag2_local);
279
280 if(kmag * Box / (2 * PI) > NYQUIST * Nsample / 2)
281 continue;
282
283 double p_of_k = PowerSpectrum(kmag);
284
285 double sign = 1.0;
286 int addr_j = j;
287
288 // special symmetries on the plane k = 0
289 if( kk == 0 )
290 {
291
292 // some points are left empty
293 if( (ii == 0) && (jj == Nmesh_2 || jj == Nmesh_2 + Nmesh_odd) )
294 continue;
295 if( (ii == Nmesh_2) || (ii == Nmesh_2 + Nmesh_odd) )
296 continue;
297
298 // the half-plane for ii > Nmesh_2 takes the seeds
299 // from points in the other half-plane
300 if( ( ii > Nmesh_2 ) ||
301 ( ii == 0 && jj > Nmesh/2 ) )
302 {
303 // symmetries on j
304 jjj = Nmesh -jj;
305 if(jjj == Nmesh)
306 jjj = 0;
307
308 if(Nmesh_odd && jj == Nmesh_2+1)
309 {
310 jjj = Nmesh_2+1;
311 addr_j = Nmesh_2;
312 }
313
314 // symmetries on i
315 if(ii > Nmesh_2)
316 iii = Nmesh - ii;
317
318
319 //unsigned int seed;
320 if(internal.large_plane)
321 // each task owns only a region of the seeds' plane
322 {
323 if(jjj == 0)
324 // the line below would read
325 // seed = SEED_y0[iii];
326 // however, since SEED_y0 starts from 0 we
327 // have to displace accordingly to i position
328 // reversed because of the requested symmetry
329 {
330 seed = SEED_y0[local_n[_x_] - i];
331 }
332 else if(iii == 0)
333 // the line below would read
334 // seed = SEED_x0[jjj];
335 // however, since SEED_y0 starts from 0 we
336 // have to displace accordingly to j position
337 // reversed because of the requested symmetry
338 {
339 seed = SEED_x0[local_n[_y_] - j];
340 }
341 else
342 {
343 map_point V;
344 V[_x_] = ii;
345 V[_y_] = jj;
346 int Q = quadrant(V , Nmesh/2) / 2;
347
348 seed = SEED_k0[Q][(jjj - SEED_k0_BL[Q][_y_]) * SEED_k0_nx[Q] + (iii - SEED_k0_BL[Q][_x_]) ];
349 }
350 }
351 else
352 {
353 // each task owns the whole seeds plane
354 seed = SEEDTABLE[jjj * Nmesh + iii];
355 }
356
357
358 sign = -1.0;
359
360 // re-initialize the spare random generator to the right seed
361 gsl_rng_set(k0_generator, seed);
362 phase = gsl_rng_uniform(k0_generator) * 2 * PI;
363 do
364 ampl = gsl_rng_uniform(k0_generator);
365 while(ampl == 0);
366
367 }
368 }
369
370 // Paired initial conditions
371 if (params.PairedIC)
372 phase += PI;
373
374 // Fixed initial conditions
375 if (!params.FixedIC)
376 p_of_k *= -log(ampl);
377
378 double delta = fac * sqrt(p_of_k);
379
380 int addr;
381
382 // calculate the storing address in local coordinates
383 if(!params.use_transposed_fft)
384 addr = 2*((i * local_n[_y_] + addr_j) * local_n[_z_] + k);
385 else
386 {
387 // the addressing here is obviously awful and inefficient because
388 // when the transposed fft are used the memory ordering is
389 // row-major in z, y, x instead of x, y, z.
390 //
391 // however the nested loops have been designed for x, y, z order
392 // that is "natural" because of the z=0 symmetries.
393 //
394 // due to this fact, for the sake of a clearer coding, we prefer
395 // not to write a different version of the loops, taking into
396 // account that this part of the code is not expèected to be
397 // dominant
398
399 if(internal.tasks_subdivision_dim > 1)
400 addr = 2*((addr_j * local_n[_z_] + k) * local_n[_x_] + i);
401 else
402 addr = 2*((addr_j * local_n[_x_] + i) * local_n[_z_] + k);
403 }
404
405 kdensity[ThisGrid][addr ] = delta * cos(phase);
406 kdensity[ThisGrid][addr + 1] = sign * delta * sin(phase);
407
408 }
409
410 }
411 }
412
413 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
414
415 gsl_rng_free(k0_generator);
416
417 if(SEED_k0[0] != NULL)
418 free(SEED_k0[0]);
419 if(SEED_k0[1] != NULL)
420 free(SEED_k0[1]);
421 if(SEED_y0 != NULL)
422 free(SEED_y0);
423 if(SEED_x0 != NULL)
424 free(SEED_x0);
425 free(SEEDTABLE);
426
427
428 /* This is needed to achieve proper normalization for pinocchio */
429
430 fac=pow((double)Nmesh, 3.0);
431 unsigned int myVectorLength = VectorLength - VectorLength % 8;
432 int i;
433 for (i = 0; i < myVectorLength; i += 8)
434 {
435 kdensity[ThisGrid][i ] *= fac;
436 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+1] *= fac;
437 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+2] *= fac;
438 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+3] *= fac;
439 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+4] *= fac;
440 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+5] *= fac;
441 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+6] *= fac;
442 kdensity[ThisGrid][i+7] *= fac;
443 }
444 for( ; i < VectorLength; i++)
445 kdensity[ThisGrid][i] *= fac;
446
447 // dump the kdensity cube on a ascii file
448 // this part shall be removed in production,
449 // or made optional
450
451 if(internal.dump_kdensity)
452 dump_cvector(kdensity[ThisGrid],
453 params.use_transposed_fft*internal.tasks_subdivision_dim,
454 MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSglobal[_x_],
455 MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSlocal_k,
456 MyGrids[ThisGrid].GSstart_k, "my.dkdensity", 0);
457
458
459 return 0;
460}

65{
66
67 /* timing */
68 cputime.init = MPI_Wtime();

72 /* this is the initialization of the random number generator */
73 random_generator = gsl_rng_alloc(gsl_rng_ranlxd1);
74

76 if (set_parameters())
77 return 1;
78

80 if (initialize_cosmology())
81 return 1;
82

85 return 1;
86

88 if (set_grids())
89 return 1;
90
91 /* computes the smoothing radii */
92 if (set_smoothing())
93 return 1;
94
95 /* now it re-initializes the variance with a top-hat filter */
96 WindowFunctionType = 2;
97 if (initialize_MassVariance())
98 return 1;
99
100 /* initializes quantities needed for the on-the-fly reconstruction of PLC */
101 SET_WTIME;
102 if (set_plc())
103 return 1;
104 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, set_plc);
105
106 /* computes the number of sub-boxes for fragmentation */
107
108 SET_WTIME;
109 if (set_subboxes())
110 return 1;
111 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, set_subboxes);
112
113#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
114 /* computes the growth rates for displacements */
115 if (set_scaledep_GM())
116 return 1;
117#endif
118
119#ifdef MASS_MAPS
120 /* initialize mass sheets (needs cosmology + outputs) */
121 if (mass_maps_init_sheets())
122 return 1;
123 /* write sheet table */
124 mass_maps_write_sheet_table();
125#endif
126
127 /* checks that parameters and directives are coherent */
128 if (check_parameters_and_directives())
129 return 1;
130
131 /* estimates the size of output file */
132 if (estimate_file_size())
133 return 1;
134
135 /* this barrier is set to have correct stdout in case the run cannot start */
136 fflush(stdout);
137 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
138
139 /* allocations of memory for fmax and memory tests */
140 SET_WTIME;
141 if (allocate_main_memory())
142 return 1;
143 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, memory_allocation);
144
145 /* initialization of fft plans */
146 SET_WTIME;
147 if (compute_fft_plans())
148 return 1;
149 ASSIGN_WTIME(partial, fft_initialization);
150
151 /* generation of initial density field */
152 if (!params.ReadProductsFromDumps) /* no generation if products are read from dumps */
153 if (generate_densities())
154 return 1;
155
156 cputime.init = MPI_Wtime() - cputime.init;
157
158 if (!ThisTask)
159 {
160 dprintf(VMSG, ThisTask, "[%s] initialization done, initialization cpu time = %14.6f\n", fdate(), cputime.init);
161 dprintf(VMSG, ThisTask, "\t\t set subboxes time = %14.6f\n"
162 "\t\t set plc time = %14.6f\n"
163 "\t\t memory allocation time = %14.6f\n"
164 "\t\t fft initialization time = %14.6f\n"
165 "\t\t density generation time = %14.6f\n",
166 cputime.set_subboxes, cputime.set_plc, cputime.memory_allocation,
167 cputime.fft_initialization, cputime.dens);
168 }
169
170 return 0;
171}
